title: REST parser drops declared HEAD operations from route registry
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the public Authentiq API OpenAPI 3.0 spec.

Spec URL:
https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/6-dot-authentiqio.appspot.com/6/openapi.json

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/6-dot-authentiqio.appspot.com/6/openapi.json -o /tmp/authentiq.json
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/authentiq.json --port 18082
```

The spec declares `head` operations for both `/key/{PK}` and `/scope/{job}`. The twin starts, but startup warnings only mention the non-HEAD operations because HEAD is silently dropped.

Repro requests:
```bash
curl -I http://127.0.0.1:18082/key/public-key
curl -I http://127.0.0.1:18082/scope/job123
```

Actual output:
```http
HTTP/1.1 405 Method Not Allowed
content-type: application/json
content-length: 45
```

Expected output:
Because HEAD is declared in the OpenAPI spec, it should not be treated as an undeclared method on an otherwise matching path. If REST twin v0 does not support HEAD execution, it should still register/classify the declared operation and return the normal structured route-refusal envelope, for example HTTP 501 `unsupported_shape` with detail `REST route is outside the CRUD route subset in v0. Path: /key/{PK}`. Startup warnings should also account for the declared HEAD operations.

Client used for the campaign:
`/tmp/authentiq_client.py http://127.0.0.1:18082` produced 4 failures, all from the two declared HEAD operations returning 405 instead of the expected declared-route refusal.

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/spec.rs` `PathItem` models `get`, `post`, `put`, `patch`, and `delete`, but has no `head` field, so serde drops OpenAPI HEAD operations during parsing. `src/protocol/rest/routes.rs` `operations()` around lines 429-446 also only enumerates those five methods, and `Method` has no `Head` variant. This is distinct from generic method-mismatch handling: the operation is declared in the spec but never enters the route registry.

title: REST GET /v1/quote returns 500 for projected json/array response columns
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the public LI.FI OpenAPI 3.0.2 spec.

Spec URL:
https://docs.li.fi/openapi.yaml

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://docs.li.fi/openapi.yaml -o /tmp/lifi_openapi.yaml
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/lifi_openapi.yaml --serve --port 18189
```

Exact curl repro:
```bash
curl -i 'http://127.0.0.1:18189/v1/quote?fromChain=1&toChain=1&fromToken=tw-test&toToken=tw-test&fromAmount=tw-test&fromAddress=0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error
content-type: application/json

{"code":"kernel_error","detail":"read results currently support only null, boolean, integer, and text-like scalar values"}
```

Expected response/output:
The startup route table advertises `GET /v1/quote` as executable (`schema-first`, resource `steps`, status `ok`). A baseline read should not return a 500. It should either serialize the safe scalar subset of the seeded row, return an empty/missing resource response, or be refused explicitly before startup/runtime execution if the response shape is unsupported.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
FAIL GET /v1/quote baseline not server error: status=500, body='{"code":"kernel_error","detail":"read results currently support only null, boolean, integer, and text-like scalar values"}'
FAIL GET /v1/quote/toAmount baseline not server error: status=500, body='{"code":"kernel_error","detail":"read results currently support only null, boolean, integer, and text-like scalar values"}'
FAIL GET /v1/status baseline not server error: status=500, body='{"code":"kernel_error","detail":"read results currently support only null, boolean, integer, and text-like scalar values"}'
FAIL GET /v1/relayer/quote baseline not server error: status=500, body='{"code":"kernel_error","detail":"read results currently support only null, boolean, integer, and text-like scalar values"}'
```

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/normalize.rs:1476-1483` projects every catalog column for REST reads via `projection_columns()`, including `json`, `bytes`, and `array` columns. The read kernel later converts projected `KernelValue`s to `ScalarValue`s in `src/kernel/read.rs:468-484` and rejects `KernelValue::Json`, `KernelValue::Bytes`, and `KernelValue::Array` with this 500-level storage/kernel error. REST already has a safe response-column filter (`src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:1818-1820`), and read projection likely needs the same safe-scalar filter or a precise route/runtime refusal.

Priority: P1 because an advertised executable GET route returns an internal server error.

title: REST mutation encoder ignores declared 204 no-content success responses
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the public Apicurio Registry OpenAPI 3.0.2 spec.

Spec URL:
https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/apicurio.local/registry/1.3.2.Final/openapi.json

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/apicurio.local/registry/1.3.2.Final/openapi.json -o /tmp/apicurio_registry.json
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/apicurio_registry.json --port 18132
python3 /tmp/apicurio_registry_client.py http://127.0.0.1:18132
```

Representative repro:
```bash
curl -i -X POST 'http://127.0.0.1:18132/rules' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"type":"VALIDITY","config":"FULL"}'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 201 Created
content-type: application/json

{"config":"FULL","type":"VALIDITY"}
```

Expected response/output:
The OpenAPI operation `createGlobalRule` declares success status `204` with no response body. The twin should honor declared no-content mutation responses when a route is classified as executable:
```http
HTTP/1.1 204 No Content
```

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
FAIL POST /rules (createGlobalRule) status: status=201, expected=[204], body={'config': 'FULL', 'type': 'VALIDITY'}
FAIL POST /artifacts/{artifactId}/rules (createArtifactRule) status: status=201, expected=[204], body={'config': 'FULL', 'type': 'VALIDITY'}
```

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/encode.rs::encode_mutation_payload()` hardcodes `201 Created` for every insert and emits a returning row whenever the kernel returns one. `RouteEntry` currently carries response fields but not the OpenAPI-declared success status or a no-content marker, so the encoder cannot distinguish POST operations that should return `204` from conventional create operations that should return `201` with a body.
close_reason: Completed: mutation encoding honors declared bodyless 204 success responses

title: REST GET /v1/accounts/{account}/people/{person} returns synthetic row for empty singleton
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the official public Stripe OpenAPI 3.0 spec.

Spec URL:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/stripe/openapi/master/openapi/spec3.json

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://raw.githubusercontent.com/stripe/openapi/master/openapi/spec3.json -o /tmp/stripe_spec3.json
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/stripe_spec3.json --serve --port 18174
```

Exact curl repro against a freshly started empty twin:
```bash
curl -i http://127.0.0.1:18174/v1/accounts/acct_test/people/person_test \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer sk_test_twinning'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
content-type: application/json

{"account":"","additional_tos_acceptances":null,"address":null,"address_kana":"","address_kanji":"","created":0,"dob":null,"email":"","first_name":"","first_name_kana":"","first_name_kanji":"","future_requirements":"","gender":"","id":"","id_number_provided":false,"id_number_secondary_provided":false,"last_name":"","last_name_kana":"","last_name_kanji":"","maiden_name":"","metadata":null,"nationality":"","object":"","phone":"","political_exposure":"","registered_address":null,"relationship":null,"requirements":"","ssn_last_4_provided":false,"us_cfpb_data":"","verification":null,"full_name_aliases":[]}
```

Expected response/output:
The backend starts empty for this spec (`No spec examples found - backend starts empty`). A singleton path with account/person identifiers should either return `404 {"code":"not_found"...}` for the missing resource or be explicitly refused if the path lookup cannot be normalized. It must not return a fabricated successful `Person` object full of default scalar values.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
singleton_empty: 33
  - GET /v1/accounts/{account}/people/{person}
  - GET /v1/accounts/{account}/persons/{person}
  - GET /v1/apple_pay/domains/{domain}
SUMMARY operations=587 assertions=1055 failures=404
```

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:1663-1684` treats any array-typed response column as a response-wrapper payload. Stripe's `person` schema has an incidental array field (`full_name_aliases`), so `component_response_wrapper()` at `src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:1431-1514` misclassifies the singleton object schema as an array wrapper and emits static default values for every other scalar field. The classifier path around `src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:683-700` then lets the response shape override the singleton relationship into a collection-style read, so empty storage encodes as a 200 wrapper object instead of a missing singleton.

Priority: P1 because this is wrong data/silent success for an empty singleton resource.

title: bd-v1r.8: Startup routing report — per-route policy attribution table
description:
## Parent epic

```rust
pub struct RouteAttribution {

```

### 2. Display format

```
METHOD  PATH                         POLICY        RESOURCE    CONFIDENCE  STATUS
------  --------------------------   -----------   ----------  ----------  ------
GET     /pets                        flat-crud     Pet         high        ok
POST    /pets                        flat-crud     Pet         high        ok
GET     /pets/{petId}                flat-crud     Pet         high        ok
```

### 3. Summary line

```
[rest] 6 routes ok (3 high, 2 pinned, 1 medium) | 0 waterfall fallbacks | 1 refused
       ⚠  1 conflict detected — see rows marked [conflict] above
```

title: REST scalar mutation response schemas are encoded as request objects
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the public OWASP crAPI OpenAPI 3.0.1 spec.

Spec URL:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OWASP/crAPI/develop/openapi-spec/crapi-openapi-spec.json

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OWASP/crAPI/develop/openapi-spec/crapi-openapi-spec.json -o /tmp/crapi_openapi.json
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/crapi_openapi.json --port 18150
```

Representative repro:
```bash
curl -i -X POST 'http://127.0.0.1:18150/community/api/v2/coupon/new-coupon' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"coupon_code":"TRAC075","amount":"75"}'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
content-type: application/json

{"amount":"75","coupon_code":"TRAC075"}
```

Expected response/output:
The declared 200 response schema is `#/components/schemas/AddCouponResponse`, a scalar string. The twin should either encode a string response shape or return a precise unsupported scalar-response refusal. It should not echo the request-body resource object as the successful response.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
FAIL POST /community/api/v2/coupon/new-coupon response shape: expected string, got dict, body='{"amount":"sample","coupon_code":"sample"}'
```

Root cause hypothesis:
Scalar response component schemas materialize as zero-field resources and are filtered out by topology/resource selection, so the route targets the request schema resource (`AddCouponRequest`). `src/protocol/rest/encode.rs:131-135` then encodes mutation `RETURNING` rows using `route.response_fields`, with no branch for scalar response schemas or a precise unsupported-response refusal.
notes: Paused/returned to open. Route-layer code in the shared worktree makes scalar component response schemas return a precise 501 unsupported scalar-response refusal, and the crAPI repro POST /community/api/v2/coupon/new-coupon returned 501 with the new detail. Full gate was not completed because shared reserved files outside this bead are currently red: bd-hepv spec.rs is incomplete/unformatted and bd-15ml topology.rs is unformatted. Do not close until the full gate runs clean after those land.

title: bd-v1r.2: CLI flags --routing and --base-prefix wired into RestArgs and RestConfig
description:
## Parent epic
bd-3j4y — Adaptive routing policy engine

## Depends on
bd-v2s6 (bd-v1r.1): RoutingPolicy enum and RoutingConfig must exist before wiring CLI

## Goal
Surface the routing policy and prefix override as first-class CLI flags so operators can twin any API without modifying the spec.

## Deliverables

### 1. `RestArgs` — add two new optional fields

File: `src/cli/args.rs` (or wherever RestArgs is defined — find with `rg 'struct RestArgs'`)

```rust
/// Routing policy: auto | flat-crud | schema-first | prefix-scoped | nested
#[arg(long, value_enum)]
pub routing: Option<RoutingPolicy>,

/// Base path prefix to strip before resource lookup (e.g. /api, /__admin)
#[arg(long)]
pub base_prefix: Option<String>,
```

Derive `clap::ValueEnum` for `RoutingPolicy` so clap renders valid variants in help text.

### 2. `RestConfig` — carry resolved config

```rust
pub struct RestConfig {
    // existing fields ...
    pub routing: RoutingConfig,
}
```

Build site (wherever RestConfig is constructed from RestArgs + spec): call `resolve_routing_config(args.routing, args.base_prefix, parse_x_twinning(&spec))`.

### 3. Thread RoutingConfig through to classify_route

`classify_route()` in `routes.rs` currently takes `catalog: &RestCatalog`. Add `routing: &RoutingConfig` parameter and pass it from the call site.

Do not implement any new classification logic here — that is Wave 3 work. Just thread the parameter through and store it on RouteEntry as `routing_config_snapshot: Option<RoutingPolicy>` (or similar) so Wave 4–7 can read it.

## Tests

- `routing_cli_override` integration test: parse a petstore spec with `routing = Some(FlatCrud)` and `base_prefix = Some("/api")`; assert resolved config has `policy = FlatCrud` and `base_prefix = "/api"`.
- `routing_clap_parse` unit test: build an arg vector with `--routing schema-first`, parse with clap, assert variant.

## Acceptance

`cargo run -- rest --help` shows `--routing` and `--base-prefix` options.
`cargo test` passes with no regressions.
`cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.

title: bd-v1r.0: ResourceTopology struct and build_spec_topology() — combine all evidence sources at startup
description:
## Parent epic
bd-3j4y — Adaptive routing policy engine

## Must run first — all other routing beads depend on this struct

## Goal

Define ResourceTopology and build_spec_topology(). This is the central data structure.
RoutingConfig (from bd-v2s6 / bd-v1r.1) is a parameter — implement AFTER bd-v2s6 defines it.

## Wave ordering note

bd-2svi and bd-v2s6 are both Wave 1, but bd-2svi depends on RoutingConfig being defined first.
Correct order: bd-v2s6 defines RoutingPolicy + RoutingConfig → THEN bd-2svi builds the topology struct.

## ResourceTopology struct — src/protocol/rest/topology.rs (new file)

```rust
pub struct ResourceTopology {
    pub path_bindings: HashMap<String, TopologyEntry>,
    pub prefix_scopes: Vec<PrefixScope>,
    pub warnings: Vec<TopologyWarning>,
}

pub struct TopologyEntry {
    pub resource_name: String,
    pub confidence: Confidence,
    pub relationship: ResourceRelationship,
    pub winning_evidence: EvidenceSource,
    pub conflict: Option<String>,
}

pub enum Confidence { Pinned, High, Medium, Low }

pub enum ResourceRelationship {
    RootCollection, RootSingleton, ChildCollection, ChildSingleton,
}

pub enum EvidenceSource { XTwinning, FlatCrud, SchemaFirst, PrefixScoped, Waterfall }

pub struct PrefixScope {
    pub prefix: String,
    pub path_count: usize,
    pub pinned: bool,
}

pub struct TopologyWarning {
    pub path: String,
    pub flat_crud_candidate: Option<String>,
    pub schema_first_candidate: Option<String>,
    pub description: String,
}
```

## build_spec_topology() — the core function

```rust
pub fn build_spec_topology(
    catalog: &RestCatalog,         // has resources, paths, and x_twinning after bd-v2s6
    config: &RoutingConfig,        // from bd-v2s6
) -> ResourceTopology
```

### Ambiguity resolution rule (specify concretely here, not just 'prefer schema-first for nested')

When flat-crud and schema-first disagree:
1. If path has more than one parameterized segment (is_nested_path): prefer schema-first, confidence=Medium
2. If flat-crud candidate IS in catalog AND schema-first candidate IS in catalog AND they differ:
   prefer schema-first, log TopologyWarning, confidence=Medium
3. If only flat-crud candidate is in catalog: use flat-crud, confidence=High
4. If only schema-first candidate is in catalog: use schema-first, confidence=High
5. If neither is in catalog: no binding (route will reach waterfall)

### Multi-prefix detection

Do NOT use 60% threshold to pick one prefix. Instead:
- Group all paths by first segment
- Any first-segment that appears in ≥3 paths AND does not match any catalog resource name = prefix scope candidate
- Build a PrefixScope for EACH such candidate (handles /v1 + /v2 coexisting)
- Pinned if config.base_prefix is Some (user/x-twinning override)

### Parent-child relationship detection

Path pattern matching:
/{resource} or /{resource}/{id} → RootCollection / RootSingleton
/{prefix}/{resource} or /{prefix}/{resource}/{id} → RootCollection in prefix scope
/{parent}/{id}/{resource} or /{parent}/{id}/{resource}/{rid} → ChildCollection / ChildSingleton

### x-twinning / CLI overrides = Confidence::Pinned

If config.policy is Some(specific) (not Auto): only run that evidence source, set Pinned.
If config.base_prefix is Some: use it as the prefix scope, set Pinned, skip auto-detection.

## Tests

- topology_petstore_high_confidence
- topology_reqres_prefix_detected
- topology_1password_nested_child: /vaults/{id}/items → ChildCollection, Item, High
- topology_multi_prefix: /v1/* and /v2/* both detected as separate scopes
- topology_conflict_logged: flat-crud says Vault, schema-first says Item → warning + schema-first wins
- topology_ambiguity_rule_nested_prefers_schema_first
- topology_x_twinning_pinned

## Acceptance

build_spec_topology() returns ResourceTopology with correct path_bindings.
All 6 tests pass. `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.

title: Freeze the representative extractor fixture and input corpus for the live canary
description:
Context:
The extractor canary is risky because it spans fixture choice, input data, and live harness behavior. The representative extractor and its deterministic fixture set should be frozen first.

Deliverables:
- one pinned extractor entrypoint or script path for the canary
- deterministic fixture/input corpus for that extractor path
- clear in-repo documentation of the subset the canary is proving
acceptance_criteria:
1. The repo contains one pinned representative extractor canary target.
2. The extractor fixture/input corpus is deterministic and checked in or otherwise reproducibly pinned.
3. The canary scope is documented tightly enough that future changes do not widen it by accident.
notes:
Target files: tests/fixtures/canaries/extractor_canary/** and extractor-canary fixture notes.
Reservation guidance: own fixture/corpus selection only.
close_reason: Pinned representative extractor entrypoint and deterministic fixture corpus with validation

title: Protect the Phase-0 bootstrap contract while v0 runtime work lands
description:
Context:
The current repo already powers working Phase-0 bootstrap pipelines. Runtime work must not silently break the existing CLI, report, snapshot, or refusal behavior while chasing shorter overall runtime. This issue is the safety roll-up for that constraint.

Deliverables:
- locked regression coverage for the current bootstrap CLI/report/snapshot contract
- explicit refusal and exit-domain coverage for current failure paths
- baseline startup/restore/reset budget capture so performance work has a reference point
acceptance_criteria:
1. Existing Phase-0 bootstrap behavior is covered by checked-in regression tests before runtime surfaces widen.
2. Current exit-domain and refusal behavior is locked by tests rather than memory.
3. A reusable budget harness exists so later runtime work can prove it did not regress the operator path.
notes:
Target files: tests/bootstrap_*.rs and tests/storage/*.rs.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via child beads, not direct code claims.

title: Implement canonical committed-state snapshot encoding and hashing
description:
Context:
Snapshot meaning is a separate contract from restore mechanics and budget measurement, so canonical encoding and hashing should land first.

Deliverables:
- canonical full-content snapshot encoding over committed state
- deterministic snapshot hashing over the declared v0 hash surface
- focused tests for excluded non-semantic fields
acceptance_criteria:
1. Identical committed state yields identical snapshot hashes across repeated runs.
2. Snapshot encoding uses full committed contents rather than overlay deltas.
3. Hash-surface exclusions are locked by focused tests.
notes:
Target files: src/snapshot/encode.rs and snapshot-hash tests.
Reservation guidance: own snapshot encoding and hashing only.

title: Implement input coercion and SQLSTATE 22P02 mapping for kernel values
description:
Context:
Type coercion is a separate failure surface from storage and constraint enforcement and should be isolated so parity failures stay legible.

Deliverables:
- coercion from client inputs into the declared runtime value model
- precise SQLSTATE 22P02 mapping for bad coercions
- focused coercion parity tests for declared types
acceptance_criteria:
1. Bad coercions surface SQLSTATE 22P02 rather than ad hoc parse errors or panics.
2. Coercion logic is reusable by insert, upsert, and later update/delete paths.
3. Coercion failures are covered by focused tests separate from storage semantics.
notes:
Target files: src/kernel/coerce.rs and coercion tests.
Reservation guidance: own coercion logic only.
close_reason: Added dedicated kernel coercion with explicit 22P02 mapping and focused parity coverage

title: REST GET /posture/integrations/{id} returns array for Tailscale singleton
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the official public Tailscale API v2 OpenAPI 3.1.0 spec.

Spec URL:
https://api.tailscale.com/api/v2

Source docs:
https://tailscale.com/docs/reference/tailscale-api
https://tailscale.com/api-docs

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://api.tailscale.com/api/v2 -o /tmp/tailscale_api_v2.yaml
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/tailscale_api_v2.yaml --serve --port 18212
python3 /tmp/tailscale_api_v2_client.py http://127.0.0.1:18212 /tmp/tailscale_api_v2.yaml
```

Exact curl repro:
```bash
curl -i 'http://127.0.0.1:18212/posture/integrations/id-1' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer twinning-test'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
content-type: application/json

[{"clientId":"93013672-b00c-4344-80ca-7ecf74f9dce1","clientSecret":null,"cloudId":"global","configUpdated":"2024-06-18T13:44:28.250168Z","id":"p56wQiqrn7mfDEVEL","provider":"intune","tenantId":"d1ae389b-5207-43a2-afca-2de6b03ac7e3"}]
```

Expected response/output:
The operation `GET /posture/integrations/{id}` declares a 200 JSON response schema `#/components/schemas/PostureIntegration`, which is an object. On empty state the twin may return a JSON 404, but a successful 200 response must be a single object, not a one-element array.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
FAIL GET /posture/integrations/{id} response shape: expected object, got list; body='[{"clientId":"93013672-b00c-4344-80ca-7ecf74f9dce1","clientSecret":null,"cloudId":"global","configUpdated":"2024-06-18T13:44:28.250168Z","id":"p56wQiqrn7mfDEVEL","provider":"intune","tenantId":"d1ae389b-5207-43a2-afca-2d'
SUMMARY operations=85 assertions=189 failures=1
```

Root cause hypothesis:
The route is schema-first (`PostureIntegration`) but the path has two literal segments plus a terminal `{id}`. `src/protocol/rest/topology.rs::path_shape_binding()` only recognizes `[literal, {id}]` as `RootSingleton`; `/posture/integrations/{id}` has no detected prefix scope and no parent path parameter, so topology falls through to `schema_in_catalog` with `ResourceRelationship::RootCollection` at `src/protocol/rest/topology.rs:338-346`. In route classification, `src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:714-740` only converts root object responses to singleton when `root_object_response_should_be_singleton()` accepts the pattern, but that helper currently only handles a single literal segment at `src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:744-758`. The route therefore executes as `ReadMany` and encodes a 200 array.

Priority: P1 because this is silent wrong response shape/data for an advertised executable GET route.

comments.author: CopperBasin
comments.text:
Fixed in this session.  now classifies as a singleton read for multi-literal terminal-param object response paths, so unknown IDs return a singleton 404 instead of a one-element collection response. Validation: Tailscale e2e client covered 85 operations / 189 assertions / 0 failures; , , and

title: REST list responses collapse OpenAPI wrapper objects into bare arrays
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the public Apicurio Registry OpenAPI 3.0.2 spec.

Spec URL:
https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/apicurio.local/registry/1.3.2.Final/openapi.json

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/apicurio.local/registry/1.3.2.Final/openapi.json -o /tmp/apicurio_registry.json
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/apicurio_registry.json --port 18132
python3 /tmp/apicurio_registry_client.py http://127.0.0.1:18132
```

Representative repro:
```bash
curl -i 'http://127.0.0.1:18132/search/artifacts?offset=0&limit=10'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
content-type: application/json

[]
```

Expected response/output:
The OpenAPI operation `searchArtifacts` declares `200` as `#/components/schemas/ArtifactSearchResults`, an object with required `count` and `artifacts` fields. An empty result should preserve the wrapper shape, for example:
```json
{"count":0,"artifacts":[]}
```
The twin should not collapse wrapper-object response schemas into a bare array.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
PASS GET /search/artifacts status: status=200, expected=[200], body=[]
FAIL GET /search/artifacts response object shape: body=[]
PASS GET /search/artifacts/{artifactId}/versions status: status=200, expected=[200, 404], body=[]
FAIL GET /search/artifacts/{artifactId}/versions response object shape: body=[]
```

Root cause hypothesis:
The REST route/encoding path currently models successful list-like reads as `RouteKind::ReadMany`, which always encodes `ReadResult` rows with `encode_read_many_payload()` as a JSON array. `RouteEntry.response_fields` captures column names but does not preserve that the OpenAPI response schema is a wrapper object with an array property plus scalar metadata (`count`). The schema-first/array unwrapping work likely selects the item resource for execution and loses the response wrapper shape before encoding.
close_reason: Completed: REST list reads preserve declared wrapper object response bodies

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: Additional e2e evidence from Adyen TransferService v3 OpenAPI 3.1 spec. Spec URL: https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/adyen.com/TransferService/3/openapi.json. Repro: cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/adyen_transfer.json --port 18133; curl -i 'http://127.0.0.1:18133/transactions'. Actual: 200 OK with [] for a route whose 200 response schema is #/components/schemas/TransactionSearchResponse, an object wrapper with data/_links. Expected: preserve object wrapper shape, e.g. an object with data array. Root cause remains the bd-2vy0 hypothesis: schema-first picks the executable item/resource path and read-many encoding collapses wrapper object responses to bare arrays.

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: Additional e2e evidence from Apideck Webhook OpenAPI 3.0.3. Spec URL: https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/apideck.com/webhook/9.3.0/openapi.json. Repro: cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/apideck_webhook.json --port 18135; curl -i 'http://127.0.0.1:18135/webhook/webhooks'. Actual: 200 OK with [] for a route whose 200 response is a GetWebhooksResponse wrapper object. Expected: preserve wrapper object shape with data array and metadata. Same root cause class as bd-2vy0: list-like reads lose OpenAPI wrapper response shape and encode bare arrays.

Actual: 200 OK with an array of ApiResponse objects for routes whose 200 response schema is the ApiResponse object wrapper. Expected: preserve ApiResponse object shape, e.g. count/message/queues fields on an object, not a bare array.

title: REST object response routes are encoded as collection arrays
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the public APIs.guru OpenAPI Directory spec.

Spec URL:
https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/apis.guru/2.2.0/openapi.json

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/apis.guru/2.2.0/openapi.json -o /tmp/apis_guru_openapi.json
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/apis_guru_openapi.json --port 18134
python3 /tmp/apis_guru_client.py http://127.0.0.1:18134
```

Representative repro:
```bash
curl -i 'http://127.0.0.1:18134/metrics.json'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
content-type: application/json

[]
```

Expected response/output:
The OpenAPI operation `getMetrics` declares `200` as `#/components/schemas/Metrics`, an object with required integer fields `numSpecs`, `numAPIs`, and `numEndpoints`. The twin should preserve object/singleton response shape, returning either a metrics object when seeded state exists or a structured 404 if absent. It should not encode the object response as a collection array.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
PASS GET /metrics.json status: status=200, body='[]'
FAIL GET /metrics.json object shape: body=[]
```

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/topology.rs:444-449` classifies any single literal path segment as `ResourceRelationship::RootCollection`, and `src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:671-680` maps `GET + RootCollection` to `RouteKind::ReadMany`. That ignores that the successful response schema is a component object (`Metrics`), so `src/protocol/rest/encode.rs::encode_read_many_payload()` emits an array for a route whose OpenAPI response is a singleton object.
close_reason: Completed: object response reads no longer serialize as collection arrays

title: Implement live runtime lifecycle, --run orchestration, and final report/snapshot boundaries
description:
Context:
The plan makes startup, shutdown, --run, and final report/snapshot boundaries explicit, but the current Beads graph does not have a top-level lifecycle issue for them. This issue owns that missing runtime shell.

Deliverables:
- startup preflight ordering and bootstrap refusal boundaries
- orderly shutdown with one final committed-state report/snapshot boundary
- live --run child-process orchestration and final artifact emission
- exit-domain handling that preserves the 0 / 1 / 2 contract
acceptance_criteria:
1. Startup preflight order matches the plan before live traffic is accepted.
2. Interactive shutdown emits exactly one final report and snapshot over committed state when configured.
3. --run captures child exit/signal/timed_out metadata without inventing a second exit taxonomy for twinning.
4. Rolled-back mutations and transient session state stay outside report/snapshot semantics.
notes:
Target files: future src/runtime.rs, signal/lifecycle helpers, and run-mode tests.
Reservation guidance: own process lifecycle here; do not absorb protocol parsing, kernel semantics, or verify meaning into this lane.

title: Implement extractor_canary harness against the live twin
description:

- focused subset assertions and failure output
acceptance_criteria:
1. Extractor fixture/corpus and live harness slices are both implemented through explicit beads.
2. The canary does not widen the claimed subset without manifest updates.
3. This issue remains a roll-up only.
notes:

Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via child beads.

The adapter also needs one normalized result contract back from the kernel before protocol work starts. This issue owns that result surface.

Deliverables:

2. Refusal results carry the fields needed for protocol-visible failures without encoding protocol frames.

Before any doctor --fix surface exists, add detector/spec/fixture coverage for twinning-specific failure modes: malformed Postgres DDL, unsupported SQL/canary shapes, catalog drift, malformed or tampered twinning.snapshot.v0 artifacts, batch-only verify artifacts in live mode, pgwire bind failures, and run_once child failure metadata. title: REST top-level array response routes return singleton 404
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the public OWASP crAPI OpenAPI 3.0.1 spec.

Spec URL:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OWASP/crAPI/develop/openapi-spec/crapi-openapi-spec.json

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OWASP/crAPI/develop/openapi-spec/crapi-openapi-spec.json -o /tmp/crapi_openapi.json
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/crapi_openapi.json --port 18150
```

Representative repro:
```bash
curl -i 'http://127.0.0.1:18150/community/api/v2/community/posts/recent'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found
content-type: application/json

{"code":"not_found","detail":"resource not found"}
```

Expected response/output:
The declared 200 response schema is a top-level JSON array of `Post`. With empty state the route should return `200 []` (or seeded post rows), not singleton 404.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
FAIL GET /community/api/v2/community/posts/recent status: status=404, allowed=[200, 405, 501], body='{"code":"not_found","detail":"resource not found"}'
```

Root cause hypothesis:
Top-level array response schemas are represented as `response_schema_ref` pointing at the item schema. `src/protocol/rest/encode.rs:185-199` treats any `ReadMany` route whose response resource equals the effective resource as a singleton response, so array-of-item responses can take the `encode_read_one_payload()` path and return 404 on empty state instead of `encode_read_many_payload()` returning an array.

title: Support ON CONFLICT updates that omit unchanged non-target columns
description:
Context:
The checked-in write differential corpus case `upsert_primary_key` currently refuses with SQLSTATE `0A000` because the IR normalizer requires every non-conflict inserted column to appear in `DO UPDATE SET`. PostgreSQL accepts omitted unchanged columns, and the checked-in corpus already depends on that shape.

Deliverables:
- allow the declared `upsert_primary_key` corpus shape to normalize and execute correctly
- preserve explicit refusal for broader unsupported ON CONFLICT shapes
- add focused regression coverage for omitted unchanged non-target columns in primary-key upserts

title: REST route classifier refuses CRUD resources behind fixed path prefixes
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the public WireMock Admin OpenAPI 3.0 spec.

Spec URL:
https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/wiremock.org/admin/2.35.0/openapi.yaml

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/wiremock.org/admin/2.35.0/openapi.yaml -o /tmp/wiremock_admin.yaml
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/wiremock_admin.yaml --port 18084
python3 /tmp/wiremock_admin_client.py http://127.0.0.1:18084
```

Representative CRUD-shaped repro requests:
```bash
curl -i "http://127.0.0.1:18084/__admin/mappings?limit=1&offset=0"
curl -i -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:18084/__admin/mappings" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{}"
curl -i "http://127.0.0.1:18084/__admin/mappings/example-id"
curl -i -X PUT "http://127.0.0.1:18084/__admin/mappings/example-id" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{}"
curl -i -X DELETE "http://127.0.0.1:18084/__admin/mappings/example-id"
```

Actual response pattern:
```http
HTTP/1.1 501 Not Implemented
content-type: application/json

{"code":"unsupported_shape","detail":"REST route is outside the CRUD route subset in v0. Path: /__admin/mappings"}
```

For item operations the detail is the same pattern with `/__admin/mappings/{stubMappingId}`. The generated client exercised all 33 WireMock Admin operations and got 35 failures, all reducible to fixed-prefix/nested admin paths being refused before the resource operation is considered.

Expected response/output:
The mappings endpoints are resource-style CRUD behind a fixed administrative prefix: collection `GET/POST/DELETE /__admin/mappings` and item `GET/PUT/DELETE /__admin/mappings/{stubMappingId}`. The REST twin should either map these to a declared `mappings`/stub-mapping resource or return a more precise unsupported-base-prefix/schema-derivation refusal. A generic outside-CRUD refusal loses the actionable distinction between true RPC endpoints and CRUD resources under a base prefix.

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:277-300` classifies only exact `/resource` and `/resource/{id}` shapes after `resource_name(pattern)` picks the first path segment. For WireMock this first segment is the fixed prefix `__admin`, so `catalog.resources.contains_key(resource_name)` fails before `mappings` can be considered. `src/protocol/rest/spec.rs:270-284` also builds resources only from `components.schemas`, while many WireMock operation schemas are inline or named differently from the path segment. A robust fix likely needs explicit base-prefix handling/path-to-resource derivation from operation request/response schema metadata, with precise refusals for paths that remain outside v0.

title: Tighten twinning.v0 schema coverage for embedded verify payloads
description:
Context:
The local `twinning.v0` schema currently constrains only `verify.version`, so machine validation under-specifies the attached embedded verify contract compared with the intended `verify.report.v1` surface.

Deliverables:
- tighten the local report schema coverage for attached verify payloads
- add regression coverage for schema-correct embedded verify sections
close_reason: Tightened local verify schema coverage and added regression checks

title: REST request-only fields skip type validation when target resource differs
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the public Bookstore REST Example OpenAPI 3.0.0 YAML spec after `bd-258j` allowed startup.

Spec URL:
https://gist.githubusercontent.com/jhigginbotham/7a1c8aa12b74c7c15fd8f053a438843d/raw/Bookstore-REST-Example_v1.0.oas3.yaml

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://gist.githubusercontent.com/jhigginbotham/7a1c8aa12b74c7c15fd8f053a438843d/raw/Bookstore-REST-Example_v1.0.oas3.yaml -o /tmp/bookstore_rest_example.yaml
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/bookstore_rest_example.yaml --port 18151
python3 /tmp/bookstore_rest_example_client.py http://127.0.0.1:18151 /tmp/bookstore_rest_example.yaml
```

Representative repro:
```bash
curl -i -X POST http://127.0.0.1:18151/carts \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"bookId\":{\"bad\":[\"type\"]},\"quantity\":1}"
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 201 Created
content-type: application/json

{"cartId":null,"cartStatus":null,"totalCostInCents":null}
```

Expected response/output:
The declared request schema `#/components/schemas/NewCart` requires `bookId` as a string and `quantity` as an integer. An object-valued `bookId` should produce a 400/422 validation error before the mutation succeeds. The same failure appears on `POST /carts/{cartId}/items` and `POST /orders` for request fields that exist in the request schema but not in the response/target resource.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
FAIL POST /carts invalid body type: status=201, body='{"cartId":null,"cartStatus":null,"totalCostInCents":null}'
FAIL POST /carts/{cartId}/items invalid body type: status=201, body='{"cartId":null,"cartStatus":null,"totalCostInCents":null}'
FAIL POST /orders invalid body type: status=201, body='{"orderId":null,"orderStatus":null,"totalCostInCents":null}'
SUMMARY assertions=31 failures=3
```

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/normalize.rs::normalize_insert()` validates required fields against `request_resource`, but `mutation_fields_from_object()` only type-checks fields that also exist as columns on the target `resource`. When schema-first routes write a response/target resource such as `CartDetails` while the request body is `NewCart`, request-only fields like `bookId` are known to the request schema but skipped during row materialization, so malformed values are ignored instead of rejected. This is distinct from inline request-body validation: the request schema here is a component `$ref`, but its non-target fields still bypass type checks.

title: Return protocol-faithful 42P01 for relations outside the declared subset
description: Reality-check follow-up for the extractor iteration story in docs/goal.md. When a client references a relation outside the declared subset, twinning should return a protocol-visible Postgres error equivalent to undefined_table (SQLSTATE 42P01) with detail that names the declared subset. This must be a client-visible pgwire error, not a process exit and not a generic unsupported_shape. Acceptance: canary fixture, simple and extended query coverage, assertion that the session recovers after Sync/Rollback as appropriate, and parity-ledger classification for this refusal.
close_reason: Returned protocol-visible undefined_table 42P01 for relations outside the declared subset across simple and extended pgwire paths, with recovery coverage.

title: Build in-memory row store and constraint executor for twinning
description:
Context:
The write kernel is a roll-up lane for value/coercion and storage/constraint/DML work. It should no longer directly depend on the full IR umbrella now that those semantic prerequisites are explicit on the leaf beads.

Deliverables:
- value/coercion lane
- storage/constraint lane
- declared write-subset parity through focused child slices
acceptance_criteria:
1. Differential write cases pass through focused child beads rather than one broad kernel patch.
2. Constraint and coercion failures surface the expected SQLSTATEs.
3. This issue remains the write-lane roll-up only.
notes:
Target files: src/kernel/** and mutation-focused differential tests.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via child beads.

title: REST POST /v1/advanced/routes advertises route but runtime-refuses target columns
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the public LI.FI OpenAPI 3.0.2 spec.

Spec URL:
https://docs.li.fi/openapi.yaml

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://docs.li.fi/openapi.yaml -o /tmp/lifi_openapi.yaml
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/lifi_openapi.yaml --serve --port 18189
```

Exact curl repro:
```bash
curl -i -X POST 'http://127.0.0.1:18189/v1/advanced/routes' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"fromAmount":"tw-test","fromChainId":1.25,"fromTokenAddress":"tw-test","toChainId":1.25,"toTokenAddress":"tw-test"}'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 501 Not Implemented
content-type: application/json

{"code":"unsupported_shape","detail":"request body schema `#/components/schemas/RoutesRequest` omits required target column(s) [fromAmountUSD, fromToken, id, steps, toAmount, toAmountMin, toAmountUSD, toToken] for resource `routes`; REST twin v0 cannot synthesize server-generated or default values"}
```

Expected response/output:
The startup route table advertises `POST /v1/advanced/routes` as executable (`prefix-scoped`, resource `routes`, status `ok`). A route that can only fail with a deterministic unsupported-shape refusal because the request body cannot materialize required target columns should be marked unsupported during classification or bound to a request/response resource that can execute consistently.

Root cause hypothesis:
The prefix-scoped classifier in `src/protocol/rest/routes.rs` binds `POST /v1/advanced/routes` to resource `routes` from the path. The request schema is `#/components/schemas/RoutesRequest`, while the response/target resource has required columns such as `id`, `steps`, and token/amount output fields that the request body cannot supply. `src/protocol/rest/normalize.rs` detects this only at runtime and returns a precise 501. This should likely be lifted into route classification for component request schemas, mirroring the earlier inline request-body target-required validation path.

Priority: P2 because this is a deterministic unsupported route being advertised as executable.

title: Build Postgres compatibility and differential test harness for twinning
description:
Context:
The twinning SQL/session surface must be defined by a real canary corpus, not a prose support table. This issue is the roll-up for compatibility harnesses, differential corpora, and the checked-in manifest-backed subset contract.

Deliverables:
- client compatibility harnesses for psql_smoke, psycopg2_params, sqlalchemy_core, and extractor_canary
- differential harnesses that compare declared write/read cases against real Postgres
- consumption of the checked-in canary manifest as the normative source of truth for supported session and SQL shapes
- explicit SKIP/refusal classification for shapes outside the manifest
acceptance_criteria:

2. The checked-in canary manifest remains the normative source of truth for the v0 SQL/session subset.
3. The manifest, harness names, and fixtures line up one-to-one.
4. Differential cases compare observable behavior against real Postgres for the declared subset.
5. Unsupported shapes are classified explicitly as protocol-visible refusal or reporting-time SKIP.
6. This issue remains a roll-up only.
notes:
Target files: tests/canaries/**, tests/differential/**, and canaries/manifest.v0.json.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via harness/corpus child beads.

title: REST singleton routes cannot use path parameters as lookup keys
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the public Nexmo External Accounts OpenAPI 3.0.1 spec.

Spec URL:
https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/nexmo.com/external-accounts/0.1.5/openapi.json

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/nexmo.com/external-accounts/0.1.5/openapi.json -o /tmp/nexmo_external_accounts.json
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/nexmo_external_accounts.json --port 18137
python3 /tmp/nexmo_external_accounts_client.py http://127.0.0.1:18137
```

Representative repro:
```bash
curl -i 'http://127.0.0.1:18137/messenger/page-123'
curl -i 'http://127.0.0.1:18137/viber_service_msg/vsm-123'
curl -i 'http://127.0.0.1:18137/whatsapp/wa-123'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 501 Not Implemented
content-type: application/json

{"code":"unsupported_shape","detail":"resource `messengeraccountresponses` has no primary key for path lookup"}
```

Expected response/output:
These are declared singleton item routes with one path parameter (`external_id`). The response schemas include a declared `external_id` field, so the twin should use the path parameter as the lookup key when no conventional `id` primary key exists, returning either a structured 404 for absent state or the declared object shape for present state. It should not refuse every item route solely because the schema lacks an `id` column.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
FAIL GET /messenger/{external_id} status: status=501, expected=[200, 404]
FAIL GET /messenger/{external_id} response shape: status=501, body={'code': 'unsupported_shape', 'detail': 'resource `messengeraccountresponses` has no primary key for path lookup'}
FAIL GET /viber_service_msg/{external_id} status: status=501, expected=[200, 404]
FAIL GET /whatsapp/{external_id} status: status=501, expected=[200, 404]
```

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/spec.rs::primary_key_for_resource()` only infers primary keys from `id`, `<schema>_id`, or required UUID fields, and does not consider operation path parameters. Then `src/protocol/rest/normalize.rs::pk_predicate()` requires `resource.primary_key` for all singleton reads/updates/deletes and refuses before it can bind the single path parameter. Route metadata already carries `RouteEntry.path_params`; a fix likely needs route/catalog primary-key override metadata derived from `{external_id}` when the resource schema contains a matching field.

title: REST remote component response refs fall through to generic outside-CRUD refusals
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the public pygeoapi demo OpenAPI 3.0.2 spec after `bd-e1co` allowed startup with remote schema-ref placeholders.

Spec URL:
https://demo.pygeoapi.io/master/openapi

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://demo.pygeoapi.io/master/openapi -o /tmp/pygeoapi_demo_openapi.json
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/pygeoapi_demo_openapi.json --port 18149
python3 /tmp/pygeoapi_demo_client.py http://127.0.0.1:18149 /tmp/pygeoapi_demo_openapi.json
```

Representative repro:
```bash
curl -i http://127.0.0.1:18149/collections
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 501 Not Implemented
content-type: application/json

{"code":"unsupported_shape","detail":"REST route is outside the CRUD route subset in v0. Path: /collections"}
```

Expected response/output:
`GET /collections` declares a 200 response as a remote component response ref:
```json
{"$ref":"https://demo.pygeoapi.io/master/schemas/ogcapi/features/part1/1.0/openapi/ogcapi-features-1.yaml#/components/responses/LandingPage"}
```
The twin should not classify the declared route as an anonymous outside-CRUD path. If v0 does not fetch remote response refs, it should return a precise `unsupported_shape` detail for the remote response ref, or eventually resolve/cache that remote response and classify from its body shape. The generic outside-CRUD message loses the declared response-ref evidence.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
FAIL GET /collections generic refusal: detail='REST route is outside the CRUD route subset in v0. Path: /collections'
SUMMARY operations=115 failures=86
```

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/spec.rs::resolve_response_object()` and `component_response_name()` only handle local `#/components/responses/...` refs. For remote response refs, `response_schema_ref()`/`response_body_shape()` in `src/protocol/rest/routes.rs` see no JSON schema/body shape and the route classifier falls through to `route_refusal(path)`. A bounded fix could detect HTTP(S) response refs and produce a precise route-level refusal instead of generic outside-CRUD; a broader fix could fetch and cache the remote response object.

title: REST POST /Files multipart/form-data reaches generic runtime 501
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the public Xero Files OpenAPI 3.0.0 spec.

Spec URL:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/XeroAPI/Xero-OpenAPI/master/xero_files.yaml

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://raw.githubusercontent.com/XeroAPI/Xero-OpenAPI/master/xero_files.yaml -o /tmp/xero_files.yaml
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/xero_files.yaml --serve --port 18160
python3 /tmp/xero_files_client.py http://127.0.0.1:18160
```

Representative repro:
```bash
curl -i -X POST http://127.0.0.1:18160/Files \
  -H "Content-Type: multipart/form-data; boundary=xero" \
  --data-binary $'--xero\r\nContent-Disposition: form-data; name="name"\r\n\r\nfile.txt\r\n--xero--\r\n'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 501 Not Implemented
content-type: application/json

{"code":"unsupported_shape","detail":"request body for resource `filess` does not declare an application/json schema; REST twin v0 cannot validate schema-less or wildcard content bodies"}
```

Expected response/output:
`POST /Files` declares a `multipart/form-data` request body and is reported as `ok` at startup. The twin should either implement the declared multipart upload enough to return the declared 201 response shape, or classify/refuse the route precisely at startup/runtime as an unsupported multipart body. It should not advertise the route as executable and then fail with a generic schema-less JSON-body refusal.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
FAIL POST /Files multipart upload: status=501, expected=201
```

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/routes.rs` classifies the operation from the response schema and does not mark non-JSON request content as an unsupported route. Later, `src/protocol/rest/normalize.rs::request_resource_schema()` and mutation normalization only support `application/json` request schemas, so the route reaches runtime before failing with the generic "does not declare an application/json schema" unsupported_shape. Route classification should record the unsupported request content type or the normalizer should emit a specific multipart unsupported-media/refusal path.

title: bd-v1r.10: Thread RoutingConfig and ResourceTopology through build_shared_state() in listener.rs
REST schema-first singleton routes can be marked ok with unmappable path parameter aliases
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the public Authentiq OpenAPI 3.0.0 spec.

Spec URL:
https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/6-dot-authentiqio.appspot.com/6/openapi.json

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/6-dot-authentiqio.appspot.com/6/openapi.json -o /tmp/authentiq_openapi.json
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/authentiq_openapi.json --port 18143
python3 /tmp/authentiq_client.py http://127.0.0.1:18143
```

Representative repro:
```bash
curl -i 'http://127.0.0.1:18143/key/PK11111111111111111111111111111111111111111'
curl -i -X DELETE 'http://127.0.0.1:18143/key/PK11111111111111111111111111111111111111111?secret=secret-1'
```

Actual responses:
```http
HTTP/1.1 501 Not Implemented
content-type: application/json

{"code":"unsupported_shape","detail":"resource `authentiqids` has no primary key for path lookup"}
```

Expected response/output:
Startup marks these routes as executable schema-first routes:
```text
GET     /key/{PK}     schema-first  authentiqids  high        ok
DELETE  /key/{PK}     schema-first  authentiqids  high        ok
```
Given empty state, `GET /key/{PK}` should return structured `404 not_found` and `DELETE /key/{PK}` should return a declared delete response or `404`, not a runtime unsupported-shape refusal. If the route is not executable because `PK` cannot be mapped to a resource column, the classifier should mark it as unsupported at startup instead of advertising it as ok.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
FAIL GET /key/PK11111111111111111111111111111111111111111 status: status=501, expected=[200, 404, 410]
PASS GET /key/PK11111111111111111111111111111111111111111 error body: body={'code': 'unsupported_shape', 'detail': 'resource `authentiqids` has no primary key for path lookup'}
FAIL DELETE /key/PK11111111111111111111111111111111111111111?secret=secret-1 status: status=501, expected=[200, 401, 404]
```

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/topology.rs:161-174` accepts the schema-first candidate `authentiqids` for `/key/{PK}` with high confidence from the inline/success response shape, and `src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:603-620` turns that topology entry into executable route kinds. But `authentiqids` has columns `devtoken` and `sub` and no primary key. At runtime, `src/protocol/rest/normalize.rs:687-718` only permits no-PK path lookup when a path parameter name can be mapped to a declared column; `PK` does not map to `sub`, so the ok route fails during normalization. The classifier and normalizer need a shared executability check for singleton path lookups, or a route-level path-parameter alias/hint mechanism that maps OpenAPI parameter `PK` to schema field `sub`.

title: Implement declared-subset live source materialization into snapshots
description: docs/goal.md says twinning should take a declared subset plus a live source connection, walk named tables, and capture committed rows into a restorable snapshot. The current implementation supports schema bootstrap/restore and fixture relation snapshots, and orchestration still refuses schema+load materialization. Add a narrow Postgres-only materialization path for declared tables in the canary subset. Acceptance: CLI or manifest surface accepts a source URL and declared tables; rows are captured in deterministic order into twinning.snapshot.v0; report/snapshot metadata records catalog declaration and source fingerprint; unsupported engines, relations, and SQL shapes refuse explicitly; focused tests cover deterministic capture and refusal behavior.
close_reason: Completed: added schema-declared Postgres source materialization via psql COPY, committed-state snapshot/report source metadata, deterministic row capture tests, and restore/source refusal coverage.

title: Route live UPDATE and DELETE mutations through the Postgres executor
description:
Finish the live mutation subset already sketched by PLAN_TWINNING and the kernel by making pgwire execution handle UPDATE and DELETE, not just INSERT/UPSERT.

Current gap:
- The semantic kernel has update/delete execution paths and tests.
- The live extended-query executor only routes Insert and Upsert; other mutation kinds are refused as unsupported mutation_kind.
- PLAN_TWINNING controlled vocabulary includes update_by_predicate and delete_by_predicate.

Scope:
- Extend live simple/extended execution to call the shared mutation executor for Update and Delete where the parser/IR recognizes the supported subset.
- Add canary manifest entries only for shapes actually proven by tests.
- Cover transaction semantics: explicit transaction, autocommit, rollback, SQLSTATE on constraint violations, and no dirty writes on failed transactions.
- Include representative RETURNING behavior only if the existing Operation IR and row contract support it cleanly; otherwise explicitly refuse RETURNING for this bead and file a separate follow-up.

Non-goals:
- No arbitrary UPDATE FROM, joins, CTEs, multi-table mutation, or broad expression support.
- No scope widening beyond predicate shapes declared in the manifest.
acceptance_criteria:
- Supported UPDATE by predicate and DELETE by predicate parse into the shared Operation IR and execute through the live pgwire path.
- The manifest names only the exact UPDATE/DELETE shapes covered by canaries.
- Live tests cover explicit transaction, autocommit, rollback, constraint violation, affected row count, and final artifact row counts.
- Unsupported UPDATE/DELETE variants return refusal envelopes or SQLSTATEs that are also covered by tests.
- Kernel and live behavior remain aligned; no duplicate mutation semantics are introduced in the protocol layer.

title: REST schema-less request bodies are validated as response resources
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the public Apicurio Registry OpenAPI 3.0.2 spec.

Spec URL:
https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/apicurio.local/registry/1.3.2.Final/openapi.json

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/apicurio.local/registry/1.3.2.Final/openapi.json -o /tmp/apicurio_registry.json
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/apicurio_registry.json --port 18132
python3 /tmp/apicurio_registry_client.py http://127.0.0.1:18132
```

Representative repro:
```bash
curl -i -X POST 'http://127.0.0.1:18132/artifacts?ifExists=RETURN' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"content":"syntax = \"proto3\";"}'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 422 Unprocessable Entity
content-type: application/json

{"code":"missing_required_field","detail":"required field `createdBy` is missing on resource `artifactmetadatas`","field":"createdBy","resource":"artifactmetadatas"}
```

Expected response/output:
The Apicurio create-artifact operation accepts schema-less artifact content (`*/*`) and returns `ArtifactMetaData`. The twin should not validate the request body as though it were the response metadata resource. It should either execute a supported content-create path or return a precise unsupported-shape refusal explaining that schema-less `*/*` content bodies are outside REST twin v0. It should not demand output/server-managed response fields such as `createdBy` from the request body.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
FAIL POST /artifacts (createArtifact) status: status=422, expected=[200], body={'code': 'missing_required_field', 'field': 'createdBy', 'resource': 'artifactmetadatas'}
FAIL POST /artifacts/{artifactId}/versions (createArtifactVersion) status: status=422, expected=[200], body={'code': 'missing_required_field', 'field': 'createdBy', 'resource': 'versionmetadatas'}
```

Root cause hypothesis:
For schema-less request bodies, `src/protocol/rest/routes.rs` leaves `RouteEntry.request_resource_name` unset. Then `src/protocol/rest/normalize.rs::request_resource_schema()` falls back to the effective route resource. On schema-first create routes, that effective resource is derived from the response schema (`ArtifactMetaData` or `VersionMetaData`), so `validate_required_for()` incorrectly applies response-required/server-managed fields to the request body.
close_reason: Completed: schema-less request bodies now refuse before response-resource validation

title: REST GET /v1/accounts/{account}/bank_accounts/{id} refuses polymorphic external_account response
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the official public Stripe OpenAPI 3.0 spec.

Spec URL:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/stripe/openapi/master/openapi/spec3.json

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://raw.githubusercontent.com/stripe/openapi/master/openapi/spec3.json -o /tmp/stripe_spec3.json
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/stripe_spec3.json --serve --port 18174
```

Exact curl repro:
```bash
curl -i http://127.0.0.1:18174/v1/accounts/acct_test/bank_accounts/id_test \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer sk_test_twinning'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 501 Not Implemented
content-type: application/json

{"code":"unsupported_shape","detail":"REST route declares scalar response resource `external_accounts`, which is not materialized as an executable REST response in v0. Path: /v1/accounts/{account}/bank_accounts/{id}"}
```

Expected response/output:
The Stripe operation has a 200 `application/json` response schema of `#/components/schemas/external_account`, where `external_account` is a polymorphic `anyOf` of `bank_account` and `card`. On an empty twin, the route should be executable and return a missing-resource 404 for `id_test`, or it should use one of the concrete variants as the response resource. It should not be classified as a scalar response just because the wrapper schema itself has no direct properties.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
get_unsupported: 79
  - GET /v1/accounts/{account}/bank_accounts/{id}
  - GET /v1/accounts/{account}/capabilities
  - GET /v1/accounts/{account}/capabilities/{capability}
SUMMARY operations=587 assertions=1055 failures=404
```

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/spec.rs:982-999` resolves `oneOf`/`anyOf` variants but `build_resource_schema()` only materializes direct `properties` from the resolved schema. For Stripe's top-level `external_account` anyOf wrapper, `src/protocol/rest/spec.rs:700-720` records a zero-field resource. Later `response_has_scalar_resource()` at `src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:1029-1045` treats zero-field response resources as scalar/unexecutable, so the executable nested GET route is turned into a 501 refusal. The fix likely needs a bounded v0 representation for polymorphic object responses (variant union, preferred concrete variant, or JSON placeholder) before route classification.

Priority: P2 because this is a missing-feature/refusal for declared OpenAPI polymorphic response schemas.

title: Composite ON CONFLICT target matching is order-sensitive
description:
Composite ON CONFLICT target matching refuses a target with the same columns in a different order than the declared UNIQUE constraint.

Contract: docs/PLAN_TWINNING.md calls out ON CONFLICT target matching and says composite keys must work. Postgres unique index inference accepts the same column set even when the conflict-target order differs from the unique constraint declaration.

Repro summary: schema declares `CONSTRAINT financials_property_period_key UNIQUE (property_id, period)`. Execute `INSERT INTO public.financials (property_id, period, noi) VALUES ($1, $2, $3) ON CONFLICT (period, property_id) DO UPDATE SET noi = EXCLUDED.noi`.

Expected: conflict target resolves to the declared composite unique surface and the upsert executes.

Actual: extended execute returns SQLSTATE 0A000 / code unsupported_shape with detail `reason=unknown_conflict_target; target=period,property_id`. Declared-order target `(property_id, period)` succeeds in the same probe.

Likely area: src/ir.rs normalize_conflict_target compares constraint.columns with resolved columns using order-sensitive equality.

Evidence from scratch probe: /private/tmp/twinning_composite_conflict_schema.sql, /private/tmp/twinning_composite_conflict_probe.py, and /private/tmp/twinning_composite_conflict_probe.json.
acceptance_criteria:
- ON CONFLICT target inference matches composite UNIQUE/PK constraints by declared column set, not only declaration order, matching real Postgres behavior for the supported subset.
- Ambiguous or genuinely unknown conflict targets still return the correct unsupported/refusal or SQLSTATE behavior.
- Add kernel/IR tests for reordered composite target, declared-order target, unknown column, and ambiguous target if representable.
- Add live pgwire parity coverage showing reordered composite ON CONFLICT succeeds and preserves deterministic final artifacts.

title: REST route matcher reports 405 for declared path-param suffix operations
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the public Google Library Agent OpenAPI 3.0.0 spec.

Spec URL:
https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/googleapis.com/libraryagent/v1/openapi.json

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://api.apis.guru/v2/specs/googleapis.com/libraryagent/v1/openapi.json -o /tmp/google_libraryagent.json
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/google_libraryagent.json --port 18091
python3 /tmp/google_libraryagent_client.py http://127.0.0.1:18091
```

The spec declares these POST operations:
```text
POST /v1/{name}:borrow
POST /v1/{name}:return
```

Use an encoded slash-bearing Google resource name so the `{name}` path parameter stays in one URL segment:
```bash
curl -i -X POST 'http://127.0.0.1:18091/v1/shelves%2F1%2Fbooks%2F2:borrow' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{}'
curl -i -X POST 'http://127.0.0.1:18091/v1/shelves%2F1%2Fbooks%2F2:return' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{}'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 405 Method Not Allowed
content-type: application/json
allow: GET

{"allowed":["GET"],"code":"method_not_allowed","method":"POST"}
```

Expected response:
Because the POST operations are declared in the OpenAPI spec and startup warnings already classify them as unsupported route-refusal shapes, runtime should match the declared route and return the normal v0 refusal envelope, for example:
```http
HTTP/1.1 501 Not Implemented
content-type: application/json

{"code":"unsupported_shape","detail":"REST route is outside the CRUD route subset in v0. Path: /v1/{name}:borrow"}
```

Client output:
```text
PASS GET /v1/shelves status: status=501, body='{"code":"unsupported_shape","detail":"REST route is outside the CRUD route subset in v0. Path: /v1/shelves"}'
PASS GET /v1/{name} status: status=501, body='{"code":"unsupported_shape","detail":"REST route is outside the CRUD route subset in v0. Path: /v1/{name}"}'
PASS GET /v1/{parent}/books status: status=501, body='{"code":"unsupported_shape","detail":"REST route is outside the CRUD route subset in v0. Path: /v1/{parent}/books"}'
FAIL POST /v1/{name}:borrow status: status=405, body='{"allowed":["GET"],"code":"method_not_allowed","method":"POST"}'
FAIL POST /v1/{name}:borrow unsupported_shape: body='{"allowed":["GET"],"code":"method_not_allowed","method":"POST"}'
FAIL POST /v1/{name}:return status: status=405, body='{"allowed":["GET"],"code":"method_not_allowed","method":"POST"}'
FAIL POST /v1/{name}:return unsupported_shape: body='{"allowed":["GET"],"code":"method_not_allowed","method":"POST"}'
FAILURES 4
```

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:119-129` only treats a path segment as a parameter when the whole segment is exactly `{name}`. A valid OpenAPI path segment such as `{name}:borrow` is parsed as a literal, so `match_route()` never matches the declared POST route. Then `src/protocol/rest/listener.rs:417-420` checks method-agnostic allowed methods; the broader `/v1/{name}` GET route matches the encoded path segment `shelves%2F1%2Fbooks%2F2:borrow`, so the listener reports `405 allow: GET` instead of the declared POST route's `unsupported_shape` refusal.

A fix likely needs path-template segment support for prefix/suffix literals around `{param}` expressions, or at minimum a precise route-refusal match for declared param-suffix paths so they do not degrade into method-mismatch responses.

title: Codify legacy-query replay boundary
description: Resolve the PLAN_TWINNING_FUTURES legacy-query boundary gap. Decide whether the first migration-proof cut replays a translated Postgres-compatible corpus against the legacy-schema twin or defers true Oracle/TNS fidelity. Acceptance: docs state the rule, fixture/query manifests encode PASS/FAIL/SKIP policy, and twinning still refuses unsupported non-Postgres protocol claims.
close_reason: Completed: codified first-cut migration replay as translated Postgres-compatible, fixture-backed the boundary, and retained non-Postgres protocol claims as deferred/refusal-only.

title: Adaptive routing policy engine — flexible multi-policy resource derivation for broad API coverage
description:
## Motivation

The v0 route classifier assumes path segment == schema name (e.g. /pets → Pet). This works for textbook petstore examples but breaks for every real-world API we tested during the e2e campaign:

- Google Books: /books/v1/volumes — prefix /books/v1 masks resource name
- 1Password: nested /vaults/{id}/items — resource is Item not vaults
- Uber: /v1/estimates/price — base prefix /v1/estimates, resource is PriceEstimate
- Reqres: /api/users — base prefix /api breaks resource lookup
- WireMock: /__admin/mappings — base prefix /__admin

All four P2 bugs (bd-331c, bd-3q08, bd-3hot, bd-3nld) share this root cause. Without this epic those APIs produce 0% route coverage.

## Solution: Adaptive routing with multiple named policies

Four named policies:
- **flat-crud**: current behavior + singular/plural normalization (pets→Pet, users→User)
- **schema-first**: derive resource from response schema $ref — ignores path entirely
- **prefix-scoped**: auto-detect fixed base prefix, strip it, then run flat-crud on remainder
- **nested**: treat inner segment as resource name for parent-scoped endpoints

Waterfall: try policies in order, accept first that classifies ≥50% of routes. Per-route attribution logged at startup.

## x-twinning OpenAPI extension

Specs can embed routing hints:
```yaml
x-twinning:
  routing: schema-first       # flat-crud | schema-first | prefix-scoped | auto
  base-prefix: /__admin       # override auto-detected prefix
  auth:
    mode: bypass              # future OAuth placeholder
```

## CLI overrides

```
twin rest --spec ./wiremock.yaml --routing prefix-scoped --base-prefix /__admin
```

Merge precedence: CLI > x-twinning extension > auto-detection

## Dependency order

Wave 1: bd-v1r.1 (core types) → bd-v1r.2 (CLI flags)
Wave 2: bd-v1r.3 (RouteEntry fields)
Wave 3 (parallel): bd-v1r.4, bd-v1r.5, bd-v1r.6 (policy implementations)
Wave 4: bd-v1r.7 (waterfall classifier)
Wave 5: bd-v1r.8 (startup report)
Wave 6: bd-v1r.9 (regression suite)

## Success metric

All 6 campaign specs (Petstore, Reqres, WireMock, Uber, 1Password, Google Books) achieve ≥80% route classification coverage.

title: bd-v1r.3: Extend RouteEntry with response_resource_name, matched_policy, effective_resource_name
description:
## Parent epic
bd-3j4y — Adaptive routing policy engine

## Depends on
bd-v2s6 (bd-v1r.1), bd-2od6 (bd-v1r.2) — RoutingConfig and RouteEntry must both be in scope

## Goal
Give RouteEntry the fields that every policy implementation in Wave 3 will populate, so classification results are fully introspectable at startup and in the report.

## Context

Current RouteEntry already has:
```rust
pub request_schema_ref: Option<String>,
pub request_resource_name: Option<String>,
pub response_schema_ref: Option<String>,
pub response_fields: Vec<String>,
```
(src/protocol/rest/routes.rs ~line 260)

The `request_resource_name` is already computed from operation schema refs but never used for classification (the classifier bails if catalog lookup misses). This wave extends the struct to record what each policy concluded.

## Deliverables

### 1. Add to RouteEntry struct

```rust
/// Resource name derived from response schema $ref (schema-first policy input)
pub response_resource_name: Option<String>,

/// Which policy successfully classified this route (None = refusal)
pub matched_policy: Option<RoutingPolicy>,

/// The resource name that will actually be used for storage and responses
/// Set by the winning policy; None if route was refused
pub effective_resource_name: Option<String>,
```

### 2. Compute `response_resource_name` at build time

In the route entry builder (same site as `request_schema_ref` computation), derive `response_resource_name` from the 200/201 response schema $ref using the existing `component_resource_name()` helper.

This is a read-only derivation — no classification logic yet.

### 3. Update all RouteEntry construction sites

Anywhere a RouteEntry is constructed (including tests), add `matched_policy: None, effective_resource_name: None`.

## Tests

- `route_entry_has_response_resource_name`: build a route entry for GET /pets with petstore spec; assert `response_resource_name == Some("Pets")`.
- `route_entry_defaults`: assert all three new fields are None before classification runs.

## Acceptance

`cargo test -p twinning` passes with no regressions.
Existing classify_route behavior is unchanged — this wave is data model only.

title: REST POST /v1/account_links rejects spec-declared form-urlencoded body
description:
Autonomous REST e2e campaign finding from the official public Stripe OpenAPI 3.0 spec.

Spec URL:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/stripe/openapi/master/openapi/spec3.json

Local repro setup:
```bash
curl -L --fail https://raw.githubusercontent.com/stripe/openapi/master/openapi/spec3.json -o /tmp/stripe_spec3.json
cargo run -- rest --spec /tmp/stripe_spec3.json --serve --port 18174
```

Exact curl repro:
```bash
curl -i -X POST http://127.0.0.1:18174/v1/account_links \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer sk_test_twinning' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
  --data 'type=account_onboarding&account=acct_test'
```

Actual response:
```http
HTTP/1.1 501 Not Implemented
content-type: application/json

{"code":"unsupported_shape","detail":"REST route declares unsupported request body content type(s) [application/x-www-form-urlencoded]; REST twin v0 only supports application/json request bodies. Path: /v1/account_links"}
```

Expected response/output:
The Stripe spec declares `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` request bodies for POST routes and a 200 JSON response for `POST /v1/account_links`. The REST twin should parse the declared form fields into the same request-resource validation/materialization path used for JSON request bodies, or at least not reject every form-backed Stripe mutation as an unsupported route. A valid form request should return the declared success status/body shape instead of a 501 route refusal.

Campaign client excerpt:
```text
form_body_refused: 292
  - POST /v1/account_links
  - POST /v1/account_sessions
  - POST /v1/accounts
SUMMARY operations=587 assertions=1055 failures=404
```

Root cause hypothesis:
`src/protocol/rest/routes.rs:1091-1126` forces any POST/PUT/PATCH operation whose resolved request body does not declare JSON into a `RouteKind::Refusal`, and the refusal text hard-codes that v0 only supports `application/json`. Stripe's official OpenAPI uses `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` for almost all mutations, so the classifier refuses the route before `src/protocol/rest/normalize.rs` can parse or validate any declared fields.

Priority: P2 because this is a missing feature/refusal for a large real-world OpenAPI 3.0 spec.
